summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a fiery show – Miracle and Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain blaze while the mix has minor cuts and a splice.
review: The performance is high‑energy, with Garcia’s voice and guitar soaring on Miracle and the band igniting on Scarlet Begonias into Fire on the Mountain. The soundboard source is generally clear and well balanced, though reviewers note a chopped start to Jack Straw and an audible splice in Fire on the Mountain, plus occasional mix quirks with Keith’s volume. Overall the show is praised as a hidden gem despite an ordinary setlist and a few keyboard missteps.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers highlight strong musical moments and good soundboard fidelity; only isolated technical glitches keep the rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Jack Straw has a noticeable cut at the start, Fire on the Mountain contains an AUD splice that is handled well. One reviewer mentions dead‑air and an echo‑y garage patch mid‑show, plus a volume clash on Keith’s solo.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid first set followed by a scorching second half; the overall pacing is praised even though some find the setlist itself ordinary.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers excellent vocals and sweet, soaring guitar runs (especially on Miracle) and is noted for three distinct solo passages.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s playing is described as pedestrian with occasional volume clashes that detract from the otherwise tight rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums and overall rhythm section are lauded as one of the world’s finest, keeping perfect tempo throughout.
